name: Grandma Cottington's Pumpkin Pie
recipeIngredient: Unsalted butter, chilled and cut into pieces (1/2 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor, pulse flour, sugar, and salt for 10 seconds.
2. Add the chilled butter and lard; process until the mixture resembles coarse meal, using on/off turns.
3. Gradually add 6 tablespoons water, mixing in enough additional water by tablespoonfuls until moist clumps form.
4. Gather dough into a ball and divide it in half. Flatten each half into disks. Wrap tightly in plastic and refrigerate for about 1 hour, or until firm (can be made up to 1 day ahead; keep refrigerated).
5. Let the dough disks soften slightly at room temperature before continuing.
6. Roll out one dough disk on a lightly floured surface to form a 12-inch round. Transfer to a 9-inch-diameter glass pie plate. Trim the overhang to 1 inch and fold edges under, crimping decoratively to form a high-standing rim. Repeat with remaining dough disk.
7. Freeze the crusts for 15 minutes.
8. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
9. Line each crust with foil and fill with dried beans or pie weights. Bake until pale golden and crisp, about 15 minutes. Let cool completely on racks.
10. In a large bowl, whisk together pumpkin puree (3 cups), sugar (1 1/2 cups), whipping cream (1 1/2 cups), milk (1 1/4 cups), flour (6 tablespoons), eggs (3), molasses (3 tablespoons), cinnamon (1 tablespoon), allspice (3/4 teaspoon), and salt (3/4 teaspoon).
11. Pour the pumpkin filling into each prepared pie crust.
12. Bake until set in the center, about 1 hour. Cool on racks.
Source: Bon Appetit 11/95
